table_name: From 1955 to 2024:A Century of Global Population

questions: How has the global population changed from 1955 to 2024, and what patterns emerge across different decades?
What is the trend in median age over the years, and how does it relate to changes in fertility rate?
How do spikes or drops in yearly population change correspond to changes in fertility rate?
Is there a visible relationship between median age and yearly percentage change in population over time?

How does the increase in population density over time correlate with global population growth?
Do periods of higher yearly percentage change in population coincide with lower or higher median ages?
Are there any noticeable anomalies in yearly population change that might signal demographic shifts?
How has the distribution of fertility rate shifted as the median age increased over the time span of the dataset?
What can be inferred about the onset of demographic transition by comparing trends in median age and fertility rate across the decades?

questions.table_name: From 1955 to 2024:A Century of Global Population
